reasoning: The value on Dec 19 was 4.56, representing a stabilization after a drop following the Fed's Dec 10 rate cut. The Fed's dot plot signaled a hawkish turn (only 1 cut in 2026), suggesting long-term Treasury yields and corporate yields will be supported moving forward. The FRED statistical tool shows a 63% probability of the yield being above 4.56 by the end of December, factoring in seasonal effects and a slightly positive medium-term trend. Spreads are currently very tight, leaving limited room for yields to fall further without Treasury yields dropping significantly (which contradicts current market forecasts of ~4.1-4.5% for the 10-year). Following the instruction to increase uncertainty (push probabilities closer to 0.5) over longer horizons, the forecasts progressively decay toward 0.5.
